subject: A war-torn city of pale stone, its towers cracked and tilting under shafts of harsh sunlight that punch through smoke and dust
description: A devastated cityscape stretches into the distance under a hazy sky. Tall, pale spires and gothic towers — some intact, some sheared in half — rise from a pile of rubble and broken masonry in the foreground. Harsh god-rays of sunlight cut diagonally through clouds of smoke and dust, bleaching the upper portion of the image and silhouetting the ruined skyline. The palette is dominated by bone-white stone, ashen grey smoke, and washed-out warm light. No figures are visible; the mood is aftermath, an empty city the morning after a catastrophe. The white-mana sun sigil sits beneath the art on a clean tan frame.
foreground: shattered masonry, broken columns, and slabs of pale stone tumbling toward the viewer
background: ruined gothic skyline of leaning spires and broken towers receding into smoke and sun-glare
